reasoning: The control SC-23(4) focuses on session authenticity with unique session identifiers and randomization, which is a withdrawn control. This type of session management is typically handled at the application layer or through specific API gateway configurations in cloud environments. Kubernetes itself does not manage session identifiers or their randomization as it is primarily a container orchestration platform. Session management is more relevant to application-level security or API management services, which are outside the direct scope of Kubernetes security controls.
title: Session Authenticity - Session authenticity | unique session identifiers with randomization [withdrawn
